cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options did NOT contribute to Nikki's reunion with her family. First, I need to go through the text again to make sure I understand all the details. Nikki was raised by her father from a very early age, and her sister was raised by her mother. They had some contact when Nikki was 6, but then communication broke down. She didn't really want to find her family while growing up, but now with a husband and son, she decided it was time. She didn't know where to start, but her husband's mum (so her mother-in-law) recommended 192.com because she had used it before. With the help of her son, Nikki searched for her sister's name on the electoral roll without a location or date of birth. The site returned two results in the area they used to live. She called the first number and it was her sister! Her sister then passed the number to their mom, who called Nikki immediately. They arranged to meet the next Saturday. Nikki reunited with her sister, met her nephews, and found out about a half-sister and brother. Now the question is which of the options didn't contribute. The options are (A) her father, (B) 192.com, (C) her son, (D) her mother-in-law. So let's break down each option: (A) her father: The father is mentioned as the one who raised Nikki from a very early age. However, there's no mention of the father playing any role in the reunion. The text says that Nikki's sister was raised by their mother, and after contact broke down, Nikki didn't seek out her family. Now, she uses 192.com with her son and mother-in-law's help. The father isn't mentioned as contributing to the recent reunion. So maybe (A) is the answer. (B) 192.com: Definitely contributed, since that's the website she used to find her sister. Without it, she might not have found her. (C) her son: She worked with her son to perform the search, so he did contribute.
